ab: miwari u harem kana wari?
ch: M:你今天有空嘛?
en: Are you available today?

ab: kemuda ziya maw?
ch: W:怎麼了?
en: What is the matter?

ab: maranger ku mukuwa muzazangiya kaninu.
ch: M:我想去拜訪你。
en: I would like to visit you.

ab: ulra ku vangavangan harem kana wari zi uniyan ku i ruma’, nu daman lra mu.
ch: W:我今天有事不在家，改天吧。
en: I am out today. How about another day?

ab: nu kemazu mu, nu ’emanan miwari u? nu alusu kadaw kayku maya’a kaninu.
ch: M:那你明天有空嗎?我下午去找你。
en: Then, are you available tomorrow? I’ll visit you tomorrow afternoon.

ab: ai, kuwa ngarayaw u i ruma’.
ch: W:可以，我在家等你。
en: Sure, I will wait for you at home.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya karuwa ku pasirep nay? maranger ku venavalray semama’.
ch: M:請問我可以錄音嗎?我想做紀錄。
en: Would you mind if  I do the voice recording? I would like to keep a record.

ab: ai, kuwatreng za eman.
ch: W:好啊，沒有問題。
en: Sure! No problem.

ab: karuwa ku semasisasing?
ch: M:可以錄影嗎?
en: Would you mind if I do the video recording?

ab: ’azi ku vulay zi amanan sasingaw ku, pawnung’azi tinulre’ay parapet na suru!
ch: W:我不好看，倉庫也沒整理，不要啦！
en: No, I am plain and the warehouse hasn’t been tidied up yet.

ab: nu kemazu mu, kuwa sasingngaw maruwa.
ch: M:那我拍照就好。
en: Then I will just take photos.

ab: amanan sasingaw.
ch: W:拍照也不要。
en: No photographing either.

ab: lrayuan nu pazuwayaw ku pakiumaumalr.
ch: M:謝謝你讓我訪問。
en: Thank you for accepting the interview.

ab: kuwatreng za eman, maranger u kiumalr za eman?
ch: W:沒問題，你想問什麼?
en: You are welcome. What would you like to know?

ab: kiumalra ku kaninu a lralrak u ziya kiyakarun u za eman?
ch: M:請問您年輕時是做什麼的?
en: What do you do when you are young?

ab: aziyu ziya kinsas ku, maw mu remetra’ ku lra harem.
ch: W:我以前是警察，但我現在退休了。
en: I was a police officer but I am retired now.

ab: harem mu ulra nu kiyakarunan?
ch: M:那你現在有工作嗎?
en: Then, are you working at the moment?

ab: sangpan ku i kungkuwan.
ch: W:我在學校上班。
en: I am working at school.

ab: kiumalra ku nannu lralrak mu ulra kaazu i zekalr?
ch: M:請問您的孩子也住在部落嗎?
en: Do your kids live in the tribe too?

ab: ’azi, nanku lralrak ulra kaazu i tokay papiya.
ch: W:不，我的孩子在都市居住。
en: No, my kids are all living in the city.

ab: nu kemazu mu, sasa u na kaazu i zekalr?
ch: M:那你自己一個人住嗎?
en: Then, do you live by yourself?

ab: ulra ku vinaaw a saya suwan zi saya ’ayam, nazu mu ninku rinuma’enan.
ch: W:我還有養一隻狗和一隻鳥，他們也是我的家人。
en: I also keep a dog and a bird. They are my family.

ab: mivinaaw ku za saya ngiyaw, ulra ini na ngiyaw ku lraalrang ’azi ku kasalrengangeseng.
ch: M:我也有養一隻貓，有動物陪伴不會無聊。
en: I have a cat too. I won’t get bored with the company of my pet.

ab: maranger ku kiumalr kaninu kanini na watralunan na palisiyan.
ch: M:我想請問你關於打獵的禁忌。
en: I would like to ask you about the taboos of hunting.

ab: ’azi ku kamazalraman, maw mu naku kinazalram kuy tuvangaw u.
ch: W:我知道的不多，但我能回答的都會回答你。
en: I don’t know much about that, but I will tell you everything I know.

ab: nanmu adain mutralun izu na petrir, na vakenan na ilrus mu ulra tu palisiyan?
ch: M:你們打獵的武器有什麼禁忌嗎?
en: Do you have any taboos about hunting weapons?

ab: nazu na adain mutralun na eman ziya mu ’azi madikes na vavayan.
ch: W:我們打獵的武器不可以讓女生碰到。
en: Our hunting weapons are untouchable to women.

ab: nu kemazu mu, na vavayan mu karuwa salrangan mukuwa mutraluna?
ch: M:那女人可以一起去打獵嗎?
en: Then, can women go hunting together?

ab: ’azi, na vavayan ’azi muwatralun.
ch: W:不行，女人也不可以打獵。
en: No, women cannot go hunting either.

ab: kana wari harem salaw ku semangal karuwa ku kiumaumalr kaninu, alra rengay ziya, kana pinakapalisi na watralunan mu?
ch: M:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: It’s a pleasure to interview you today. Would you please talk about the taboos of hunting?

ab: ai. a lralrak ku ziya mu marayas ku muzenan paalrupa.
ch: W:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: Sure, I used to go hunting very often when I was young.
